backlog_trend_metrics.note: First post-PROP-030 entry — queue-history.jsonl was empty until this run. WoW comparison N/A until at least one prior entry exists.

findings.description: Assigned-analyst queue depth (150) trips PROP-030 major-tier threshold (>100); open_issues_total (207) trips moderate-tier threshold (>200). Net 7-day velocity is mildly positive (+6, closing 60 / week vs opening 54 / week) so burndown is making progress, but the load has shifted from status='open' (132 → 48 since 2026-05-10) into status='assigned-analyst' (83 → 150). The analyst pipeline is now the bottleneck. PROP-031's open-bucket BAU triage (deployed 2026-05-11T19:54) will accelerate routing INTO the analyst queue, which may worsen the assigned-analyst tier reading before it improves.
findings.evidence: Computed in pre-flight: open_issues_total=207 (moderate-tier threshold >200); assigned_analyst_count=150 (major-tier threshold >100; operator_escalation threshold is strictly >150 so we are at-but-not-over). Trajectory from daily-reports: status='open' 132 (05-10T17-21) → 48 (05-11T17-24) — net drain via M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST. Assigned-analyst 83 → 150 over same window confirms the load shift. analyst-tracker shows EXP-302..323 (22 expansions in 48h) — analyst is producing, but not fast enough to keep ahead of M1+M2 routing.
findings.suggested_fix: Watch for two consecutive runs of assigned_analyst >150 (operator_escalation tier). If hit, route via HNOTE to operator: consider either (a) bumping analyst cadence to 2h during burndown (currently 4h), or (b) tightening Priority 3b's per-item processing budget so ROUTE-TO-ANALYST is reserved for items requiring genuine analyst judgment (vs items decider could WONTFIX or NARROW-PATCH itself). Do NOT escalate now — net velocity is positive and PROP-031 hasn't had a full BAU cycle yet.

findings.description: Decider total context-load grew +23.8% (561 lines added) since 2026-05-09 baseline, dominated by decider.md itself (+47.9%, 520L → 769L) and decider-curmudgeon.md (+60.1%, 306L → 490L). This exceeds the >10% MAJOR-alert threshold from tinker-cost-engineering.md Step 4 and approaches the >25% threshold. Drivers are PROP-027 (routing matrix, ~150L new + decider edits), PROP-028 (stale-assessment overwrite guard, Mech A/B), PROP-029 (M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST tracker creation), PROP-030 (backlog-trend monitoring, mostly tinker.md not decider), PROP-031 (open-bucket BAU triage + cap removal). Each PROP was justified individually; the cumulative load is not.
findings.evidence: Per-file measurements 2026-05-12: decider.md=769L, decider-intake.md=313L, decider-curmudgeon.md=490L, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md=594L, decider-reporting.md=201L, BUILD-AND-CHANGE.md=121L, SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT.md=69L, DATA-SCHEMAS.md=184L. Sum (with CLAUDE.md=179L) = 2920L vs prior baseline 2359L. Also note: the Step 4 measurement script EXCLUDES routing-matrix.md (194L, new), state-verification.md (174L), and state-file-archives.md (108L) which the decider's dispatcher routes to conditionally — actual context load when those branches fire is 3320-3400L+, which the Step 4 figure understates by ~15%.
findings.suggested_fix: (1) Extend the cost-engineering Step 4 measurement script to enumerate the full dependency closure of decider.md (grep for 'reference/.*\.md' references in the dispatcher and sum reachable files), so the metric stops understating actual load. (2) Open a cost-engineering PROP for decider-curmudgeon.md (currently 490L) — it accumulated the M1+M2+M3 carry-over logic + Priority 3b sweep + routing-matrix decision tree references; extract Priority 3b's procedure to a separate decider-priority-3b.md so dispatcher loads it conditionally. (3) Audit decider.md (769L) for 'always-loaded but only used in one priority path' content — the dispatcher should keep routing logic, push heavy procedure to reference files. Both fixes are PROP-shaped, not self-fix territory.
recommended_prop_targets: Step 4 measurement script extension (full closure walk)

findings.description: Curmudgeon total context-load grew +11.0% (101 lines) since 2026-05-09 baseline (915L → 1016L). curmudgeon.md (426L → 456L) and DATA-SCHEMAS.md (124L → 184L) account for the bulk; the conditional curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md is unchanged at 128L. DATA-SCHEMAS.md is shared with analyst+decider+integrity so the 60L growth multiplies. Crossing 10% triggers a moderate flag but is not urgent — curmudgeon's per-run output (3-5 reviews/run, no no-ops in last 15 runs) is high-value and the growth was driven by schema documentation for PROP-027 routing-matrix + PROP-028 stale-assessment + PROP-030 backlog-trend.
findings.evidence: curmudgeon.md=456L, SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT.md=69L, DATA-SCHEMAS.md=184L; dispatcher+refs sum 709L (vs prior 619L = +90L = +14.5%). Conditional 128L unchanged. With CLAUDE.md=179L, max_total 1016L vs prior 915L = +101L = +11.0%.
findings.suggested_fix: Audit DATA-SCHEMAS.md for sections only one consumer reads — if a schema is only relevant to the decider's curmudgeon-queue handling, move it to a decider-specific reference file rather than the shared schemas doc. Defer to a Mode 4 PROP next run after the decider deep-dive (T-2026-05-12-02) is sized.

findings.description: Root partition at 95% used (9.0G / 9.6G; 542M available). Project-induced footprint 878MB, of which 405MB is /tmp/edit-clone (mtime 2026-05-11T18:17, age 10h). Empowerment rule for /tmp/*-clone cleanup requires age ≥ 24h (10h not eligible) AND /tmp/edit-clone specifically is excluded if age < 2h (10h passes that exclusion but fails the global 24h floor). NOT taking action this run. Operator should be aware: if disk crosses the 'failing clone' threshold during the overnight window before /tmp/edit-clone ages out to 24h+, decider's next clone may fail (the 2026-05-07→08 failure mode).
findings.evidence: df -h /: 9.0G/9.6G (95%), 542M available. du -sm: /sessions/zen-exciting-ramanujan/mnt = 472MB, /tmp/edit-clone = 405MB, /tmp/dome-monitor.py = 1MB. Total 878MB exceeds WARNING (500MB) but under CRITICAL (1000MB). Empowerment safety rules require mtime ≥ 24h AND glob-match — 10h fails the first gate.
findings.suggested_fix: Operator-side: when next interactive session ends, rm -rf /tmp/edit-clone (or let it age to 24h+ at which point next tinker run is auto-cleanup-empowered). No agent-side action.

findings.description: PROP-031 (just landed 2026-05-11T19:54) and PROP-028 (landed 2026-05-11T18:18) have no verification_pattern field. Per tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md Step 2 rules ('Always include a verification_pattern field'), future tinker runs cannot auto-verify these without manual file-reads. The cautionary tale of PROP-003 (re-escalated 9 consecutive runs after being fixed because md5-match passed for the wrong reason) explicitly calls this out.
findings.evidence: node check: status='implemented' for both, verification_pattern=undefined for both. PROP-027/029/030 all have verification_pattern strings; 026 has a multi-condition narrative pattern (returns empty when shell-evaluated, but documented manually).
findings.suggested_fix: Self-fix: append verification_pattern fields to PROP-028 and PROP-031. For PROP-028, a viable pattern is `grep -q 'rejectStaleEdit\|stale_assessment_guard' build-scripts/digest-reviews.js test.js monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN` (need to confirm exact symbol from the patch). For PROP-031, viable pattern is `grep -q 'Priority 3b' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md && grep -q 'open.bucket.bau.triage\|open-bucket-bau-triage' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`. Both can be added as additive edits without touching any other PROP fields. **Deferred to operator review** — adding verification_pattern to an existing PROP after status=implemented is a mutation, not an additive edit by the documented exception rules; the cleaner fix is a new PROP-032 that adds the field with explicit operator approval, OR the operator decides this is a one-off retro-fix and edits the files directly.

findings.description: CLAUDE.md schedule table (lines 138-145) describes the four core agents with 'Variable (NNm churn-and-burn / Nh quiet)' patterns, but the live cron expressions are fixed-cadence: analyst at `50 */4 * * *` (every 4h), curmudgeon at `30 3,7,11,15,19,23 * * *` (every 4h), decider at `10 2,6,10,14,18,22 * * *` (every 4h), tinker at `30 3 * * *` (daily). The 'churn-and-burn' framing reflects the 2026-04-26 era; cadence was normalized to fixed 4h on 2026-04-27 (decider) and stayed that way for curmudgeon. CLAUDE.md's table understates the cadence stability and overstates operator-side dynamic-scheduling effort.
findings.evidence: list_scheduled_tasks output: cronExpression fields for dome-analyst, dome-curmudgeon, dome-decider, dome-tinker. CLAUDE.md lines 138-145. dome-tinker daily cron `30 3 * * *` would be roughly 04:30 BST (1h shift) or local-equivalent, not '10:30 AM' as CLAUDE.md says.
findings.suggested_fix: Update CLAUDE.md schedule table to reflect actual fixed-4h cadences. Replace 'Variable (...)' descriptions with '4h fixed (cron N */4 * * *) — bumped to 30m churn-and-burn manually when needed'. Update tinker row to 'Daily at 03:30 UTC' from '10:30 AM'. This is a low-risk mechanical edit and qualifies for self-fix under the 'fix stale references in prompts' rule. **However**, CLAUDE.md is the highest-impact document and the cron expressions could change again — recommend operator-review on the precise wording before commit. Deferring to Mode 4 next run with a concrete PROP if no operator pushback by then.
findings.fix_skipped_reason: Defer to operator-review pass — CLAUDE.md edits multiply across all 8 agents; would prefer to bundle with the decider context-load PROP (T-2026-05-12-02 fix) since that's the next CLAUDE.md-touching change.

findings.description: Decider no-op rate on 2026-05-10 morning: 5 consecutive runs (05:19, 07:19, 07:37, 07:58, 10:54) with patches=0, props=0, closed=0, opened=0. Each run still clones the repo (~290MB), reads prompts, and exits. The 14:22 run resumed substantive activity (7 patches). This is the exact 'morning quiet window' wasted-Opus pattern PROP-031 is designed to address — Priority 3b sweep gives the decider work to do every run rather than tap-out when the digest is empty. Worth tracking the no-op rate over the next 7d to validate PROP-031's effect.
findings.evidence: Decider activity scan, last 10 daily-reports: 5/10 are NO-OP runs (all clustered 2026-05-10 morning UTC). 5/10 are substantive (patches+closures). Pre-PROP-031 baseline no-op rate ~50%. PROP-031 validation criterion is 'oldest_open_item_age_days <24h consistently within 2 weeks'; we should also explicitly track no-op rate as a secondary metric.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a tinker tracking metric — record per-run decider patches/closures/proposals_processed in queue-history.jsonl alongside the open-issues counts. Next Mode 3 run computes 7d no-op rate and compares to pre-PROP-031 baseline. No PROP needed yet; can be added to the next CLAUDE.md/tinker-cost-engineering.md update.

performance_findings: CLAUDE.md size 179L multiplies across all 8 agents — +11L since 2026-05-09 = +88 reader-lines aggregate. Growth driven by PROP-031 + PROP-030 ownership additions to the File Ownership table. Each addition is justified individually; cumulative growth bears watching.
The append-only-edit exception language for the tinker (L88-92) is one of the longest paragraphs in the file. Worth extracting to reference/agent-edit-exceptions.md if the file grows past 200L.

(c) assigned-analyst not blowing past 150 from PROP-031's increased throughput INTO the analyst queue. If (c) trips operator_escalation tier (>150), consider analyst cadence bump.

Operator decides whether to retro-fit or accept the gap and tighten the 'always include verification_pattern' rule going forward.
Refresh CLAUDE.md schedule table (L138-145) to reflect actual fixed cron cadences. Bundle with PROP-032 context-load PROP for atomic operator review.

Disk pressure: project footprint 878MB / 95% root partition. /tmp/edit-clone is 10h old — not yet auto-cleanup eligible. Operator should consider rm -rf /tmp/edit-clone at end of next interactive session, or let it age to 24h+ for tinker auto-cleanup.

counter_arguments_considered.claim: Decider context-load growth of +23.8% is the cost of doing business — five PROPs landed.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Each PROP was justified, but cumulative load isn't. The pattern is real: every PROP adds ~50-100L 'somewhere' and reviewer-attention drifts to the next PROP before extraction. PROP-032 (recommended) is the structural answer — preserve PROP gains, shed the unnecessary dispatcher load via extraction to conditionally-loaded references. Counter to this counter: extraction-PROPs themselves accumulate. Don't pretend extraction is free; budget it explicitly.

counter_arguments_considered.claim: PROP-031 may worsen analyst backlog before it improves; we should have waited.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Net velocity (+6/wk) shows burndown is working but slow. The 7-21d cohort (M1 sweeps) was the bottleneck; PROP-031 widens the throughput channel. If analyst backlog tips operator_escalation tier (>150 sustained, 2 consec runs), we cadence-bump analyst. That's a known follow-up, not an unmitigated risk. Backing off PROP-031 to preserve a slow steady-state would leave the 7-21d 'rot until M1 catches it' bug uncorrected, which is what triggered the directive in the first place.

counter_arguments_considered.claim: The 50% decider no-op rate is morning-window-specific and would self-correct without intervention.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Even if true (5/5 NO-OPs are clustered 05:19-10:54 on 2026-05-10), that's still 5 Opus invocations wasted on one calendar day. Sustained over a week, that's ~35 wasted runs. PROP-031 routes those runs to Priority 3b sweep instead — same Opus cost, but useful output. Counter: could also be solved with Haiku pre-flight gate (cheaper). But Haiku doesn't have the judgment to do the routing-matrix work, and PROP-031 explicitly captures that judgment work. Both can coexist later if needed.
